about.p: Iryna Okur — writer, author of the books «Recipes of the Lviv Lady from the Shores of the Bosphorus» and «You Will Hear Me Now», author of the first Ukrainian-language Instagram blog in Istanbul @iryna.okur. Born in Lviv, she graduated from Ivan Franko Lviv National University with a degree in Philology. By profession, she is a teacher of Ukrainian language and literature. She is fascinated by Ukrainian dialects, living language, and how it changes in migration and daily life. Since 2017, she has been living in Istanbul, where she writes, teaches Turkish, and steadily expands the presence of Ukrainian culture in everyday life. Her texts work with themes of female experience, corporeality, adaptation, intercultural differences, and social taboos.
book.title: ...If someone wrote a funny cookbook in the dialect of my native Karadeniz region, I’d pay a good amount of money for it — even though I don’t cook.
book.title.author: — Nazım
book.h2: “Recipes of the Lviv Lady from the Shores of the Bosphorus”
book.p: «Recipes of the Lviv Lady from the Shores of the Bosphorus» is Iryna Okur’s debut book, in which cuisine becomes a language of memory, humor, and cultural dialogue. These are stories about life between Lviv and Istanbul, about a Ukrainian woman in Turkish reality, about family, adaptation, and self-irony. The book combines recipes, essays, and observations, capturing everyday life, language, and character with precision and ease — without embellishment, but with warmth.
book.figcapture.second: The "Lazy as Your Cat" Napoleon Cake
book.figcapture.third: The Ugly Freak Cake, or Why I Never Became a Pastry Chef
books.title: Every surgeon carries within himself a small cemetery, where from time to time he goes to pray.
books.title.author: René Leriche
books.p: «You Will Hear Me Now» is a literary novella that grew out of the experience of living between two cultures and attentively listening to women’s stories. It is a book about how a woman is shaped in her family, society, and her own body — under the influence of traditions, fears, and silent prohibitions. Without moralizing — instead with precision, honesty, and inner tension.
books.h3: When Your Body Is a Territory of Foreign Power
books.h3.third: When the Ottoman Empire Still Stood
books.presentation: On November 30, the book “Will You Hear Me Now” was presented for the first time at the İstiklal National Library (İstiklal Millet Kütüphanesi) in Istanbul, located at Şahkulu district, Meşrutiyet Cd., No. 121, 34430 Beyoğlu.
